candidates.notes: pilot_h1_h4: H4 Spearman r=0.647 exceeds 0.3 threshold. H1 preliminary signal: layer_8 absorption > layer_4. pilot_h3: Low-absorption features show 80.6% larger steering effects (ratio=1.81). pilot_h2: PARTIAL GO — TopK SAE achieves 70.9% absorption reduction but 8x worse MSE; JumpReLU fails to converge. pilot_h5: Did not strictly pass (4.95% < 5% threshold) but shows consistent directional trend: high-absorption features perform worse on BOTH simple (AUC 0.636 vs 0.710) and causal tasks (AUC 0.522 vs 0.547). The task-dependence delta of 4.95% suggests absorption degrades downstream discriminability, though the causal task is more noisy. Full H5 with better datasets may yield stronger results.
